questions.question: Which approach best detects suspicious behavior across identity, endpoint, and network telemetry?
questions.description: XDR correlates signals across multiple security layers to surface higher-confidence detections.

questions.question: Why are salts used with password hashes?
options: To prevent reuse of precomputed hash tables
questions.description: A unique salt makes identical passwords produce different hashes and reduces rainbow-table effectiveness.

questions.question: Which metric describes the average time needed to detect an incident?
questions.description: Mean Time To Detect measures detection speed after suspicious or malicious activity begins.

questions.question: Which control helps prevent OAuth consent phishing?
questions.description: Admin consent workflows and publisher verification reduce unauthorized OAuth application grants.

questions.question: Which evidence most strongly indicates command-and-control beaconing?
options: Regular outbound callbacks to a rare domain
questions.description: Periodic outbound communication to uncommon infrastructure is a common C2 indicator.
